message:
feat(search/model): policy-driven semantic model acquisition + model-cache classification

Lift the ad-hoc "is the ONNX model present?" / "can we download?"
logic in model_manager.rs into a single typed `ModelAcquisitionPolicy`
plus a `ModelCacheState` classifier driven by the existing
SemanticPolicy, CliSemanticOverrides, and ModelDownloadPolicy in
src/search/policy.rs. Gives `cass search --mode=semantic` a coherent
story for why acquisition is or isn't happening and surfaces the
reason back to the user instead of silently falling back to hash
embeddings.

src/search/fastembed_embedder.rs
- `MODEL_ONNX_SUBDIR` + `MODEL_ONNX_LEGACY` flip from crate-private
  to `pub` so ModelAcquisitionPolicy construction in model_download
  can enumerate candidates without a second source of truth.
- `FastEmbedder::select_model_file` promoted from private to `pub`
  and reimplemented on top of a new public helper
  `model_file_candidates() -> &'static [&'static str]` so callers
  can also iterate candidate locations (model_manager needs this
  for the "which variant did we pick?" diagnostic).

src/search/model_download.rs
- New `ModelAcquisitionPolicy` struct with explicit typed fields
  capturing every knob that can block acquisition:
    downloads_enabled, requires_consent, offline, metered,
    allow_metered, max_model_bytes, mirror_base_url, config_source
  Serde-serialisable so the robot surface can echo the active
  policy in tracing spans and diagnostic JSON.
- `Default` keeps downloads_enabled=true / offline=false / … so
  tests can construct a "happy path" policy cheaply.
- New `ModelCacheState` enum + `classify_model_cache(model_dir)`
  helper that distinguishes:
    Ready { variant } — ONNX present, tokenizer present, all good.
    MissingModel { missing_files } — partial install.
    Empty — never downloaded.
    Corrupted { reason } — required file exists but fails a sanity
      probe (zero-size / unreadable).
  Variant selection matches FastEmbedder's preference order
  (modern onnx/ subdir over legacy flat).
- `pub use` re-exports `ModelAcquisitionPolicy`, `ModelCacheState`,
  `classify_model_cache` so downstream modules can import from one
  place.

src/search/model_manager.rs
- Previous import of `ModelManifest, ModelState, check_version_mismatch`
  expands to include `ModelAcquisitionPolicy, ModelCacheState,
  classify_model_cache`.
- Also pulls in `CliSemanticOverrides, SemanticPolicy` from
  src/search/policy.rs so load_semantic_context_inner can weave
  the resolved policy through its decision tree.
- `load_semantic_context_inner` rewritten to:
    1. Call classify_model_cache(&model_dir) once.
    2. Branch on the returned ModelCacheState.
    3. For MissingModel, return the existing
       SemanticAvailability::ModelMissing but with the typed missing
       files list from the classifier.
    4. For Corrupted, return a new availability variant (in callsite
       already wired, see enum match exhaustiveness).
    5. For Ready, fall through to the existing version-mismatch
       probe, now driven by the same ModelManifest.
- Net effect: the logic is the same in the happy path; the new
  paths expose reasons through the SemanticAvailability enum
  instead of hiding them in Result::Err strings.

No behaviour change in `cass search --mode=lexical` — the new code
only executes when semantic is selected or tentatively considered
during hybrid fallback.
